video:video.video:title: Tides of War
video:video.video:description: Song from the Mataala: Nalani narrative. The colonial-era arrival, read through the strategist who sees the war drifting in before others do.

video:video.video:title: My Island School
video:video.video:description: Song from the Mataala: Nalani narrative. Mission education turned into reconnaissance. The girl who learned the colonizer's language to use it back.

video:video.video:title: Born for Storms
video:video.video:description: Song from the Mataala: Nalani narrative. The character anthem. Refusing the categories the mission filed her under.

video:video.video:title: Silent Watcher
video:video.video:description: Song from the Mataala: Nalani narrative. The quiet read. Watching before acting.

video:video.video:title: Tides of Blades
video:video.video:description: Song from the Mataala: Nalani narrative. The night the strategist drops the paperwork and brings steel. Force when force saves her people.

video:video.video:title: MATAALA
video:video.video:description: Song from the Mataala: Nalani narrative. The island itself, the constraint that shaped the conflict.

video:video.video:title: Mataala: Nalani — Hero Reel
video:video.video:description: The indigenous strategist Nalani and the rogue trader Turnbull. Hero reel for the Mataala: Nalani narrative.

video:video.video:title: Nalani — Character Study
video:video.video:description: Nalani: indigenous strategist who reads terrain, law, and timing against the colonial-era merchants arriving on her shores.

video:video.video:title: Elias Turnbull — Character Study
video:video.video:description: Elias Turnbull: rogue trader and principal antagonist in the Mataala: Nalani narrative.

video:video.video:title: Redfern — Character Study
video:video.video:description: Redfern: Turnbull's overseer, caught between two worlds.

video:video.video:title: Hale — Character Study
video:video.video:description: Hale: pragmatist whose alignments shift with the strategic calculus.
